introSection: Dvergbanen (previously known as Den aller minste) is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at TusenFryd in Vinterbro, Oslo, Norway. A sign on the ride claims it is the "smallest roller coaster in the world".

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride has the standard Take Off 041 layout. It's trains are not the usual planes however.

sections.title: Name
sections.content: The name "Dvergbanen" means "Miniature Railway" in Norwegian, and "Den aller minste" translates to "The Smallest". Although this ride has often been called Teeny-weeny, there has never been any signage saying this, so this may be an unofficial name.

fullText:
Dvergbanen (previously known as Den aller minste) is a steel kiddie roller coaster located at TusenFryd in Vinterbro, Oslo, Norway. A sign on the ride claims it is the "smallest roller coaster in the world".

The ride has the standard Take Off 041 layout. It's trains are not the usual planes however.



The name "Dvergbanen" means "Miniature Railway" in Norwegian, and "Den aller minste" translates to "The Smallest". Although this ride has often been called Teeny-weeny, there has never been any signage saying this, so this may be an unofficial name.
